Fix a crash when submitting forms with Client Actions (@sebmarkbage [#33055](https://github.com/facebook/react/pull/33055)) - Hide/unhide the content of dehydrated suspense boundaries if they resuspend (@sebmarkbage [#32900](https://github.com/facebook/react/pull/32900)) - Avoid stack overflow on wide trees during Hot Reload (@sophiebits [#34145](https://github.com/facebook/react/pull/34145)) - Improve Owner and Component stacks in various places (@sebmarkbage, @eps1lon: [#33629](https://github.com/facebook/react/pull/33629), [#33724](https://github.com/facebook/react/pull/33724), [#32735](https://github.com/facebook/react/pull/32735), [#33723](https://github.com/facebook/react/pull/33723)) - Add `cacheSignal` (@sebmarkbage [#33557](https://github.com/facebook/react/pull/33557)) #### React DOM - Block on Suspensey Fonts during reveal of server-side-rendered content (@sebmarkbage [#33342](https://github.com/facebook/react/pull/33342)) - Use underscore instead of `:` for IDs generated by `useId` (@sebmarkbage, @eps1lon: [#32001](https://github.com/facebook/react/pull/32001), [https://github.com/facebook/react/pull/33342](https://github.com/facebook/react/pull/33342)[#33099](https://github.com/facebook/react/pull/33099), [#33422](https://github.com/facebook/react/pull/33422)) - You can log Owner Stacks when debugging or use Owner Stacks to enhance error overlays or other development tools. Owner Stacks are only available in development builds. Component Stacks in production are unchanged. * An Owner Stack is a development-only stack trace that helps identify which components are responsible for rendering a particular component. An Owner Stack is distinct from a Component Stacks, which shows the hierarchy of components leading to an error. * The [captureOwnerStack API](https://react.dev/reference/react/captureOwnerStack) is only available in development mode and returns a Owner Stack, if available. findings.description: Repository contains a proper LICENSE file with complete MIT License text and clear copyright attribution to Meta Platforms, Inc. and affiliates.
findings.evidence: File: LICENSE (SHA: b93be90515ccd0b9daedaa589e42bf5929693f1f) - Full MIT License text present with copyright notice: 'Copyright (c) Meta Platforms, Inc. and affiliates.'
findings.recommendation: Maintain current LICENSE file. No changes needed.

findings.description: Source files consistently include standardized copyright and license headers referencing MIT license and pointing to root LICENSE file.
findings.evidence:
Search query 'repo:facebook/react "MIT License" OR "Copyright (c) Meta" path:packages/react/src' returned 62 files with headers. Sample from packages/react/src/ReactAct.js (SHA: 1e74d222a29aba4e0c2710341a66ef7523981303) shows header: '/**
 * Copyright (c) Meta Platforms, Inc. and affiliates.
 *
 * This source code is licensed under the MIT license found in the
 * LICENSE file in the root directory of this source tree.'
findings.recommendation: Continue using consistent header format across all source files. Excellent practice.

findings.description: Root package.json is marked as private (monorepo workspace root) and does not declare a license field. Individual packages in workspaces should have their own license declarations.
findings.evidence: File: package.json (SHA: 0ae925a1d4ecd7389a21d953ed05e151c9b03dbc) shows '"private": true' and '"workspaces": ["packages/*"]' but no license field at root level.
findings.recommendation: Verify that each publishable package in packages/* includes '"license": "MIT"' in its package.json. Root private package.json does not require license field, but published packages do.

findings.description: Development dependencies are standard JavaScript ecosystem tools with permissive licenses compatible with MIT. No copyleft or incompatible licenses detected.
findings.evidence: package.json devDependencies include MIT-licensed tools: @babel/* packages, eslint, prettier, jest, rollup, typescript, and other standard build tooling. No GPL or restrictive licenses observed.
findings.recommendation: Continue monitoring dependency licenses during updates. Current dependency set is fully compatible.

findings.description: No NOTICE or COPYING files present. These are not required for MIT-licensed projects as the license requirements are satisfied by the LICENSE file and source headers.
findings.evidence: Repository tree query showed files: LICENSE, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, README.md, CHANGELOG.md, but no NOTICE or COPYING files. MIT license only requires copyright notice in copies of the software, fulfilled by LICENSE file and source headers.
findings.recommendation: No action required. NOTICE files are typically required only for Apache 2.0 or when documenting third-party attributions. Current approach is compliant for MIT.

findings.description: Repository demonstrates exemplary license clarity with consistent MIT licensing across all observable components. Copyright holder clearly identified as Meta Platforms, Inc. and affiliates.
findings.evidence: LICENSE file (SHA: b93be90515ccd0b9daedaa589e42bf5929693f1f) contains full MIT text. All 62+ source files examined contain consistent copyright headers. Repository tree shows clear documentation structure.
findings.recommendation: Maintain current exemplary practices. This repository serves as a model for open-source license compliance.
